tipRules.tip: Yamamoto: 'Prefers weightless Texas rig with 3/0 Gamakatsu EWG hook. Some people use too big of a hook - they lose the action on the fall and the bait tears off'
Yamamoto: 'Smaller light-wire hooks increase tail action and make Senko last longer since bait slides easier up hook onto line while fighting fish'
Yamamoto: 'Recommends rigging Senko on a Yamamoto Split Shot Hook (circle hook) with a snell knot - when you tug it pulls the hook barb straight toward the line'

knowledge.insight: Yamamoto: 'One of the best colors that is probably a sleeper is salt-and-pepper. I think it is a forgotten color, but I can take that color any place in the world and fish will bite it'
Yamamoto: 'Employs a simple lift-and-drop retrieve - it is like working a plastic worm but not quite as fast because it takes longer for it to fall'
Yamamoto: 'Prefers 3/0 Gamakatsu EWG on weightless Texas rig. Smaller light-wire hooks increase tail action and bait durability'
